title: Crisis Communication Draft
instruction:
A PR incident has generated multiple email threads. Find and read the following:
(1) The original customer complaint from theo.rivera@customer.com with subject containing "Unacceptable Data Delivery Delays".
(2) The internal legal advice email from Maya Rivera with subject containing "Legal Guidance — Project Horizon Data Delay".
(3) The two draft response threads from the comms team. Find the two draft response emails: one from Miles Brooks with subject "Draft Response v1 — Project Horizon Data Delay" and one from Priya Rivera with subject "Draft Response v2 — Project Horizon Data Delay". Identify which draft was approved by Sofia Brooks — exactly one draft's thread contains a reply from Sofia Brooks with the phrase "This draft is approved for external use". That is the approved draft. The other was rejected.

Then perform all of the following actions:
(a) Compose a new email to theo.rivera@customer.com only (no CC, no BCC) with subject "Re: Unacceptable Data Delivery Delays" using the body text from the approved draft as the response.
(b) Forward the legal guidance email to jordan.singh@company.com.
(c) Star the approved draft email and archive the rejected draft email.
eval_check_descriptions: External reply sent to complainant only, using approved draft body
Legal guidance forwarded to comms lead
Rejected draft is archived (removed from inbox)

resolved_targets.draft_a_subject: Draft Response v1 — Project Horizon Data Delay
resolved_targets.draft_b_subject: Draft Response v2 — Project Horizon Data Delay
resolved_targets.approval_phrase: This draft is approved for external use
resolved_targets.approved_snippet: capacity bottleneck in our processing pipeline
resolved_targets.rejected_snippet: expect the situation to be fully resolved within the next 48 hours

emails.subject: Re: Draft Response v1 — Project Horizon Data Delay
emails.body:
APPROVED — use this response. I think this version is better and more empathetic. Go ahead and send it to the customer.

Thanks,
Theo

emails.subject: Executive Directive — Project Horizon Data Delay
emails.body:
This situation is a top priority. Ensure the approved response goes out today. Do not send anything without the designated approver's sign-off.

Thanks,
Sofia

emails.subject: Re: Draft Response v1 — Project Horizon Data Delay
emails.body:
Do not use this version. It references a 48-hour timeline which legal has not approved, and the tone is too tentative. Please defer to the other draft.

Thanks,
Sofia

emails.subject: Re: Draft Response v2 — Project Horizon Data Delay
emails.body:
This draft is approved for external use. Good work on this version — it aligns with legal guidance and strikes the right tone. Please proceed with sending to the customer.

Thanks,
Sofia

emails.subject: Draft Response v1 — Project Horizon Data Delay
emails.body:
Hi team, here is an alternative draft for the Project Horizon Data Delay response:

Dear valued customer,

Thank you for bringing the data delivery issue to our attention. We are aware of the delays affecting Project Horizon deliverables and our team is actively working on a resolution. We expect the situation to be fully resolved within the next 48 hours and will provide a detailed post-mortem once complete. We appreciate your patience during this time.

Please let us know if you need any interim data exports.

Let me know your thoughts.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Draft Response v2 — Project Horizon Data Delay
emails.body:
Hi team, here is my draft response for the Project Horizon Data Delay customer communication:

Dear valued customer,

We sincerely apologize for the delays in data delivery related to Project Horizon. Our engineering team has identified the root cause — a capacity bottleneck in our processing pipeline — and has implemented a permanent fix as of this morning. We are committed to ensuring this does not recur and have added dedicated monitoring. As a gesture of goodwill, we are extending your current service tier at no additional cost for the next quarter.

Please do not hesitate to reach out if you have further concerns.

Please review and let me know if this is ready to send.

Thanks,
Priya

emails.subject: Updated rollout checklist
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

Nothing looks blocked right now, but we still need one decision on timing. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Marcus

emails.subject: Follow-up on venue estimate
emails.body:
Sharing the latest notes before tomorrow's check-in.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Theo

emails.subject: Customer Escalation — Project Horizon Data Delay
emails.body:
Escalating the Project Horizon Data Delay situation. The customer has been in contact with our support team daily and is growing increasingly frustrated.

They mentioned potential legal action in their last call.

Thanks,
Nina

emails.subject: Follow-up on venue estimate
emails.body:
Sharing the latest notes before tomorrow's check-in.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Legal Guidance — Project Horizon Data Delay
emails.body:
Hi Avery,

Regarding the Project Horizon Data Delay situation, here is our legal guidance:

1. Do not admit fault explicitly — use language like 'we identified an issue' rather than 'we caused the problem.'
2. Offer a concrete remediation (service credit, extended tier) rather than vague promises.
3. Do not reference the post-mortem timeline externally until we finalize it internally.
4. Any external communication must be approved by the designated approver before sending.

Thanks,
Maya

emails.subject: Agenda draft for Monday check-in
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

Nothing looks blocked right now, but we still need one decision on timing. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Elena

emails.subject: Customer feedback from pilot cohort
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

Nothing looks blocked right now, but we still need one decision on timing. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Priya

emails.subject: Revised budget worksheet
emails.body:
Sending a quick status update from today's working session.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Unacceptable Data Delivery Delays
emails.body:
To whom it may concern,

We have been experiencing severe delays in data delivery for Project Horizon. This is now impacting our downstream operations and client commitments. We need an immediate explanation and remediation plan.

This is unacceptable and we are considering escalating to our legal team.

Thanks,
Theo

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.
